description_long: This is a double-reed woodwind instrument, specifically a bassoon, featuring a long, cylindrical wooden body with a dark brown finish. The instrument is adorned with an intricate array of metallic keys, rods, and levers, primarily in silver and gold tones, running along its length. The top end is fitted with a thin, protruding bocal (crook) that holds the reed, while the bottom end flares slightly. The keywork is dense and complex, with multiple finger plates and posts, indicating a fully keyed mechanism for advanced playability. The surface of the wood appears polished and smooth, with the metallic parts providing a glossy, reflective contrast. No visible branding or decorative engravings are present, and the instrument is shown without a carrying case.
description: A dark brown, polished bassoon with intricate silver and gold keywork and a protruding bocal at the top. The instrument features a smooth wooden body and dense, glossy metallic keys.
description_view_0: Two bassoons side by side, highlighting the contrast between silver and gold keywork on the dark wooden bodies.
description_view_1: A direct front view showing the full length of the bassoon, with the bocal and reed at the top and the dense keywork running vertically.
description_view_2: Side-by-side view emphasizing the metallic keywork and the cylindrical shape of the instrument, with both gold and silver finishes visible.
description_view_3: A rear view showing the back of the bassoon, with the keywork less prominent and the smooth, dark wood surface more visible.
